content: I'm so grateful for my friends and family. They've been really supportive of my decision to become a high school math teacher.
That's wonderful. It's great to have a supportive network. What inspired you to become a math teacher?

I've always loved math, and I had some amazing teachers when I was in school. I want to be able to make a similar impact on my students. My sister, Rachel, is actually a teacher too, and she's been a great resource for me.
That's terrific. It's wonderful that you have a role model in your sister. Do you have any favorite math topics to teach?

I really enjoy teaching geometry and algebra. There's something about seeing the concepts click with my students that's really rewarding. I've also been trying to incorporate more real-world applications into my lessons, like using astronomy to teach about orbits and trajectories.
That sounds like a great approach. Making math relevant to students' lives can really help them understand and appreciate the subject. Do you have any hobbies or interests outside of teaching?

I'm actually really into archery right now. I've been practicing for a few months, and I'm thinking about competing in a local tournament. My roommate, Chris, is into drone photography, and he's been helping me get some great shots of my archery practice.
That's a unique hobby. Archery can be really challenging and rewarding. Do you have a favorite type of bow or shooting style?
I've been using a recurve bow, and I really like it. I've also been experimenting with 3D printing to make my own archery accessories, like arrow rests and quivers.
That's really cool. 3D printing can be such a versatile tool. Have you thought about sharing your archery experiences with your students?

Actually, yes. I've been thinking about starting an archery club at school. I think it could be a great way to teach my students about focus, discipline, and teamwork. My colleague, Michael, is a ski coach, and he's offered to help me get the club off the ground.
That sounds like a wonderful idea. Extracurricular activities can be so beneficial for students. I'm sure your archery club will be a hit. Do you have any plans for the summer break?

I'm actually thinking about taking a trip to the mountains to go skiing and hiking. I love spending time outdoors, and it's a great way to clear my head. Maybe I'll even get some good stargazing in, since I'm really into astronomy.
That sounds like a fantastic way to spend your summer break. Getting out into nature can be really rejuvenating. Don't forget to bring your binoculars for stargazing.
I will, thanks. I've actually been thinking about getting a telescope to take my astronomy hobby to the next level. Do you have any recommendations for a good beginner telescope?
There are many great options out there. I'd be happy to help you research and find the perfect telescope for your needs. It was great chatting with you about your teaching career and hobbies.
